affected_groups: Students and families currently enrolled in integrated 1-8 non-state schools
Central government / ministry designing the structural reform
content.decision_question: Institutional/organizational autonomy of non-state maintainers versus central government's claimed authority to impose uniform structural standards across the school system.
content.evidence_boundary: Contested/unknown: whether the scenario as currently specified includes or excludes an opt-out mechanism for non-state maintainers is not documented in the available scenario text; this is a gap in the policy design record rather than a resolved factual question, and the maintainer's claim is a conditional objection pending clarification.
content.tension: A structural split between lower and upper grades imposed centrally overrides the curricular and organizational choices of non-state (church-maintained) schools that wish to retain an integrated 1-8 structure, without any documented opt-out provision.
content.title: Decision tension A9: S2
value_poles: central government's claimed authority to impose uniform structural standards across the school system.
